name: Azure Artifacts
cost: Free tier: 2 GiB storage per organization. Beyond that, consumption-based tiered per-GiB storage billing starting at ~$2/GiB (2-10 GiB band) and decreasing to ~$0.25/GiB at the 1,000+ GiB band; enabled by setting org billing to "No limit, pay for what you use". Bundled with Azure DevOps, whose Basic plan is free for the first 5 users then ~$6/user/month. Self-hosted Azure DevOps Server requires separate Azure DevOps Server / client-access licensing.
repo: https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/azure-devops-docs (documentation only; the product itself is closed-source)
perf_notes: Per-file size limits: 500 MiB for NuGet, npm, Maven, Python, and Cargo; 4 TiB for Universal Packages (npm additionally caps package.json at 375 KB). Count limits: 5000 versions per package ID, unlimited package IDs per feed, 20 upstreams per package type per feed. No published throughput/latency or memory benchmarks (it is an opaque managed service).
scale_notes: Primarily a fully managed multi-tenant SaaS (Azure DevOps Services) with Microsoft-operated HA and redundancy; storage is auto-scaled and billed by usage. Also self-hostable as Azure DevOps Server 2022 (requires SQL Server; supports multi application-tier deployments for HA). Feeds can be organization- or project-scoped. Universal Packages are Services-only; Cargo requires Server 2022+.
summary: Microsoft's managed package-management service inside Azure DevOps: multi-ecosystem private feeds (Python, NuGet, npm, Maven, Cargo, Universal) with on-demand PyPI upstream proxying and deep Azure Pipelines / Entra ID integration, offered as SaaS and as self-hosted Azure DevOps Server.
extras: Multi-ecosystem feeds: a single feed can host Python, NuGet, npm, Maven, Cargo and Universal Packages side by side
Universal Packages: store arbitrary files (any type) up to 4 TiB per file (Azure DevOps Services only)
Views (@local / @prerelease / @release) provide promotion and release-gating workflows
Upstream sources can chain other authenticated Azure DevOps feeds, not only public registries (PyPI, npmjs, NuGet.org, Maven Central)
On-demand upstream caching: first install from PyPI is saved into the feed, protecting against upstream outages and yanks
Cross-organization feed access via Microsoft Entra workload identity federation (OIDC), eliminating PATs
artifacts-keyring plugin / Azure Artifacts Credential Provider for transparent pip/twine/uv auth
Recycle bin with permanent tombstone records: a deleted version's name+version is reserved forever and cannot be republished to the same feed
Public feeds: share packages publicly with anonymous read (all types except Universal Packages)
Dependency-confusion safeguards: feed-local versions take precedence over upstream, with controls over allowing externally-sourced versions
Tight Azure Pipelines integration via PipAuthenticate@1 / TwineAuthenticate@1 tasks using the build service identity
Download/usage metrics available via the versionmetricsbatch REST API
